sutta: buddha said timely for action is the world timely for action is the world thus say the unlearned manifold or ordinary vote but they know not when it is timely or untimely monks there are these 8 untimely unseasonable occasions for living the holy life take the case monks when the tata arises in the world an arahant fully awakened 1 replete with practice and knowledge a welfare world know incomparable tamer of tameable men teacher of devas and men an exalted 1 and dhamma is taught which is tranquilizing cooling leading to awakening declared by the welfare and suppose a person arises in hell this month is the first untimely unseasonable occasion for living the holy life again suppose a tatargata arrives and dhamma be taught and a person arises in the animal kingdom or among the ghosts betas or among the long-lived deva community or in the outlying countries among unintelligent barbarians where there is no scope for monks nuns for lay disciples male or female or in the middle countries but he holds wrong views he is perverted in vision and things that gifts offerings and sacrifices are as known that the fruit and result of good and bad deeds done are as not that this world does not exist nor the next world that there are neither mothers nor fathers nor beings born spontaneously nor any recluses of holy men in the world who have found the highest who have 1 to the highest who make declaration of this world and the next after realization by personal knowledge or in the middle countries but he is foolish and dull a witness imbecile unable to distinguish whether a matter has been spoken well or ill this monks is the seventh untimely unseasonable occasion for living the holy life moreover monks take the case when no tata gather arises in the world dhamma is not declared and a person is born in the middle country and is intelligent and not dull nor are witness in desire but able to tell whether a matter has been spoken well or ill this monks is the eighth untimely and seasonable occasion for living the holy life these monks are the 8 occasions monks there is just 1 occasion which is timely seasonable for living the holy life but 1 take the case monks when the tata gather arises in the world and arahant fully awaken 1 replete with practice and knowledge welfare world knower incomparable of tameable men teacher of devas and men an exalted 1 and dhamma tranquilizing cooling leading to awakening declared by the wealth error is taught and a person is born in the middle country and is intelligent not dull nor with less imbecile but can tell whether a matter has been spoken well or ill this monks is just the 1 occasion which is timely seasonable for living the holy life that's the end of the sutta
commentary: [Music] this this oh saying that if the buddha arises in the world and dhamma is taught and available but if a person arises in the wrong places like arise is born in hell or in the animal world kingdom or among the ghosts or among the long-lived devas or in the outlying countries then there is no scope for practice of the holy life the outlying countries means those countries out of the middle region or middle country where the dhamma is available this middle country the pali word is majima padesa i think and it refers to the area around the kanjis river valley because the buddha used to live and travel around this area the ganges valley and so if you are born out of this area then you have no chance to know the dhamma or if a person is born in the middle country but he holds wrong view and in the wrong wrong view here is stated to mean uh he he does not believe that making offerings gifts and sacrifices are of any use that the there is no result of good and bad deeds that this world or does not exist the next world does not exist there are no beings born spontaneously um there are no holy men who have realized the highest realization etc [Music] or if a person is born in the middle country but he's foolish and dull and cannot understand the dhamma or no buddha arises in the world and there's no dhamma to be heard even though a person is born in the middle country these conditions are not suitable for living the holy life there's just 1 occasion which is suitable that is when a buddha rises in the world and dhamma is declared and a person is born in the middle country that was spoken during the time of the buddha but nowadays it is also a very suitable time to live the holy life because a buddha has arisen in the world and dhamma is available to us especially here now almost all of the sutas of the buddha are available to us in book form and and so um we can practice the holy life especially like we are in this country where there are monks and nuns and lay supporters and if a person is intelligent enough to understand the dhamma then we can practice the holy life so we are considered very lucky we are actually considered to be born during the era the time of the buddha because the dharma is still available to us and if we listen to the dhamma or read the book sir it is as though the buddha is just at our side speaking the dhamma to us the next suta is also very good and important suta
